highlights.text: I wrote on Wednesday at 4pm: “Bear case tomorrow: There is a possible short on the failure of 6954….
I wrote Thursday at 4pm: “My general lean is after today’s pullback ES can try some backtesting.
As I discuss at length it typically does two things: Firstly ES goes elevator down .
These short squeezes are caused by one thing: My core setup, the Failed Breakdown.
We saw the full gamut last week starting with the elevator down sell.
When does ES go Elevator Down ?
For the 6954 short one wants to see a final bounce there, then one can short perhaps 6950.” Yesterday morning we broke the range down and got an elevator down sell into 6808 by Friday morning.
The task for bulls Friday (CPI day) was to put in a Failed Breakdown and try to rally and back-test some of the zones that broke down yesterday.
On Friday morning around the open, ES put in a monster Failed Breakdown, and the short squeeze began.
The task for bulls today was again to put in another Failed Breakdown, this time (ideally) of Friday’s daily 6808 low.

I wrote on Friday at 4pm: “If this is the case, the obvious entry is to wait for 6795 to defend (doesn’t need to be exact) then recover today’s 6808 daily low.” This morning ES flushed down to 6791, recovered 6808, and we ripped hard to 6860+ again.
In today’s newsletter I’ll talk this, I’ll go over today’s Failed Breakdowns (these are key to know), and I’ll discuss the actionable plan for tomorrow.

post_title: Huge Volatility in SPX To Go Nowhere. Is a Move Coming? February 18th Plan
